policy.notes: This catalog is a discovery queue only. Candidates are not installed, enabled, or trusted until a separate capability-intake slice pins versions, scans supply chain risk, defines sandboxing, wires observability, and validates rollback.

candidates.name: LangGraph
candidates.maturity_signal: Large active orchestration project with durable execution, memory, human-in-the-loop, and tracing surfaces.
candidates.why_research: Closest parity target for graph-based workflow execution and recursive self-improvement loops.
parity_checks: Compare state graph primitives to config/workflow-blueprints.json.
Map interrupts and checkpoints to local delegation resume files.
Identify dashboard metrics needed for graph execution visibility.
candidates.intake_action: Research API patterns before any dependency import.

candidates.name: Google Agent Development Kit
candidates.maturity_signal: Open-source code-first Python framework with workflow runtime, fanout/fanin, retry, state, and human-in-the-loop primitives.
candidates.why_research: Good parity target for local workflow runtime behavior and evaluation/deployment lifecycle.
parity_checks: Compare workflow graph semantics to local workflow DSL.
Check whether ADK eval patterns improve aq-qa coverage.
Map agent lifecycle hooks to hybrid coordinator routes.
candidates.intake_action: Extract lifecycle and evaluation patterns only.

candidates.name: OpenAI Agents SDK Python
candidates.maturity_signal: Lightweight multi-agent framework with handoffs, tools, MCP, guardrails, sessions, tracing, and realtime agent concepts.
candidates.why_research: Useful parity target for agent handoff contracts, guardrails, tracing, and MCP tool exposure.
parity_checks: Compare handoff semantics to local role contracts.
Compare guardrails to capability-intake and reviewer gates.
Map tracing fields to aq-report and dashboard metrics.
candidates.intake_action: Research design patterns and tests before any SDK adoption.

candidates.name: Microsoft AutoGen and Agent Framework
candidates.maturity_signal: AutoGen has a large installed base, but the repository indicates maintenance mode and migration toward Microsoft Agent Framework.
candidates.why_research: Use as migration-pattern research, not as a direct enablement target.
parity_checks: Identify durable multi-agent conversation patterns.
Compare MCP and A2A alignment in the successor framework.
Flag deprecated patterns to avoid.
security_gates: no runtime enablement without successor review

candidates.name: CrewAI
candidates.maturity_signal: Active role-playing agent orchestration project with recent releases.
candidates.why_research: Comparable role/task crew model for local multi-agent collaboration and workflow blueprints.
parity_checks: Compare role/task declarations to docs/architecture/role-matrix.md.
Map crew planning to local workflow blueprints.
Identify reusable test fixtures for delegated task execution.

candidates.name: OpenHands
candidates.maturity_signal: Large self-hosted coding-agent control center supporting local, remote, and cloud backends.
candidates.why_research: Direct parity target for coding agent control planes, sandboxing, and agent backend adapters.
parity_checks: Compare backend adapter model to delegate-to-local and switchboard profiles.
Identify sandbox defaults and unsafe modes.
Map observability and session replay features.
candidates.intake_action: Research architecture and sandbox constraints; no install.

candidates.name: Aider
candidates.maturity_signal: Mature terminal pair-programming agent with a large user base.
candidates.why_research: Useful parity target for repo editing loops, git-aware coding flows, and minimal CLI UX.
parity_checks: Compare git-aware edit workflow to Codex/local-agent handoff.
Assess commit hygiene and test retry patterns.
Identify small CLI affordances to add to aq-workflow.
candidates.intake_action: Mine workflow patterns and tests only.

candidates.name: Continue
candidates.maturity_signal: Prominent open-source coding-agent project, but the repository reports it is no longer actively maintained.
candidates.why_research: Can inform IDE integration patterns, but should not drive new implementation until successor status is clear.
parity_checks: Extract stable config/schema ideas only.
Avoid stale extension dependencies.
Compare user-facing model/provider config patterns.
candidates.intake_action: Defer; research docs only if needed for IDE parity.

candidates.name: Browser Use
candidates.maturity_signal: Large browser-agent project focused on making websites accessible to AI agents.
candidates.why_research: High-value parity target for website creation research, browser-assisted QA, and OSINT collection workflows.
parity_checks: Compare browser session model to Playwright MCP.
Identify allowed-domain and credential handling practices.
Map browser traces into OSINT evidence records.
candidates.intake_action: Research guarded browser-agent patterns; do not enable autonomous browsing by default.

candidates.name: Model Context Protocol Servers
candidates.maturity_signal: Official reference implementations and pointers to the MCP registry.
candidates.why_research: Baseline for MCP server discovery, reference implementations, and registry parity.
parity_checks: Compare local MCP catalog to official reference and registry categories.
Identify missing server classes useful for repo, memory, time, filesystem, and fetch flows.
Flag educational-only reference servers before production use.
candidates.intake_action: Use as registry baseline and security comparison source.

candidates.name: mcp-use
candidates.maturity_signal: Full-stack framework for developing MCP apps and servers.
candidates.why_research: Potential parity target for MCP app composition and server developer experience.
parity_checks: Compare MCP app/server abstraction to local MCP builder guidance.
Assess schema and auth patterns.
Identify reusable validation ideas for MCP integrations.
candidates.intake_action: Research SDK ergonomics; no runtime enablement.

candidates.name: Mem0
candidates.maturity_signal: Large memory layer project for AI agents with temporal and entity-linked retrieval concepts.
candidates.why_research: Direct parity target for local agent memory, AIDB knowledge stores, and self-improvement loops.
parity_checks: Compare episodic/semantic/procedural memory model to local stores.
Map retrieval scoring to AIDB query traces.
Identify privacy and deletion controls.
candidates.intake_action: Research memory algorithms and privacy controls before implementation.

candidates.name: Letta
candidates.maturity_signal: Stateful agent platform focused on advanced memory and self-improvement.
candidates.why_research: Useful parity target for long-lived agent state, tool memory, and learning loops.
parity_checks: Compare stateful agent object model to local memory tiers.
Map self-improvement controls to reviewer gates.
Identify observability for memory mutations.

candidates.name: LlamaIndex
candidates.why_research: Parity target for document ingestion, query engines, and RAG evaluation.
parity_checks: Compare ingestion pipeline abstractions to OSINT research store.
Identify query evaluation fixtures.
Map document provenance into evidence records.
candidates.intake_action: Research ingestion and eval patterns only.

candidates.name: SWE-agent
candidates.maturity_signal: Software engineering agent project oriented around benchmarked repo work.
candidates.why_research: Useful for comparing coding-agent loop structure, benchmark harnesses, and failure remediation.
parity_checks: Compare task loop to local delegate-to-local agent mode.
Map benchmark signals to aq-qa.
Identify issue reproduction and patch validation patterns.

candidates.name: CAMEL
candidates.why_research: Potential source for society-of-agents patterns and task decomposition comparisons.
parity_checks: Compare role-play and workforce abstractions to local role matrix.
Identify reusable delegation metrics.
Check alignment with local security boundaries.

candidates.name: AutoGPT Platform
candidates.maturity_signal: Long-running AI automation platform with broad community awareness.
candidates.why_research: Useful as a historical and UI/workflow parity comparison, not a first implementation target.
parity_checks: Compare agent automation UI patterns.
Identify failure modes and sandbox boundaries.
Extract only mature, still-maintained patterns.
